definition: Represent each site, range, or ecological sample by environmental variables in climate or niche space, then compare overlap or novelty using metrics such as Schoener's D, Hellinger-based niche overlap, MESS, MOP, or distance in environmental principal-component space. For SPECTRA, this audits whether ecological and environmental models generalize to non-analogous climates or low-overlap niche regions.
example_usage: python tabular_mahalanobis_similarity.py --train train_climate.csv --eval eval_climate.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --feature-cols-prefix bio_

citations.title: Assessing climate niche similarity between persian fallow deer areas in Iran

evidence.summary: The paper assesses climate niche similarity and frames niches as environmental-space hypervolumes.
It computes environmental overlap with Schoener's D and discusses MESS, MOP, and ExDet for analogous environments and extrapolation.
The authors interpret near-zero climate overlap as substantial dissimilarity between origin and new areas.

limitations: Environmental similarity depends on variable selection, spatial resolution, temporal baseline, and accessible-area definition.
Niche overlap metrics can mix sampling availability with biological tolerance.
Geographic proximity and environmental similarity are different axes and should not be conflated.
quality_gates: Report climate variables, resolution, temporal period, accessible-area mask, scaling, and extrapolation method.
Run separate curves for geographic distance and environmental niche similarity when both matter.
Check non-analogous conditions and variable-selection sensitivity.
